synthesis.summary: HOOD is down 13% on 3x normal volume with no high-impact news in the feed to explain the move. ARK Invest buying $39M worth of shares is a bullish signal that runs counter to the sell-off, suggesting the drop is driven by factors not captured in the supplied news — likely broader market risk-off flows, options activity, or institutional selling pressure.

Mastercard surged 3.5% on more than double normal volume following a strong earnings report showing robust core business growth, with additional attention drawn to its expanding stablecoin strategy. The news confirms fundamental momentum, but a 3.5% move with this volume spike likely also reflects broader market flows or options activity beyond the headline alone.

GEHC dropped 13% on 10x relative volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The oversold RSI (24.3) and multi-day loss of ~17% suggest a significant fundamental catalyst — likely an earnings miss, guidance cut, or macro sector development — but none is present in the provided news feed to confirm.

NXPI surged ~25.6% on 3.77x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. This magnitude of gain — pushing RSI to 81 — typically requires a major catalyst such as an acquisition bid, blowout earnings, or large guidance revision, none of which appear in the available news feed.

STX surged 11.15% on 2.5x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The magnitude, volume spike, and overbought RSI (78.3) suggest a catalyst exists — likely earnings, a sector rotation, or options-driven flow — but nothing in the available news confirms it.

TER dropped ~19% today on volume more than 5x normal with a bearish MACD cross and RSI near oversold territory, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. This magnitude of selling with this volume profile typically points to an earnings miss, guidance cut, or major fundamental event — none of which are visible in the provided data.

Visa is up 8.3% on 3.6x normal volume, but the only supplied news covers Mastercard's business and stablecoin strategy — a competitor, not Visa itself. The move may reflect sector-wide payments tailwinds lifting both networks, but no Visa-specific catalyst is present in the supplied news to explain the magnitude or timing.

EXTR surged 28% on 3x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The magnitude, volume spike, and overbought RSI (76.8) suggest a catalyst exists — likely an earnings beat, M&A rumor, or large options-driven squeeze — but none of those can be confirmed from available inputs.

ESI surged ~9.75% on 3.4x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The spike could reflect options flow, short covering, or sector rotation, but none of those mechanisms are confirmed by available data.

ETSY is up 10% on 2.5x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The magnitude and volume spike suggest options activity, short covering, or sector/peer rotation rather than any identifiable company-specific catalyst.

FCEL surged 37% on 3.5x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. This has the hallmarks of a short-squeeze or speculative options-driven spike — FCEL has historically been a heavily shorted small-cap fuel cell name — but no fundamental catalyst is identifiable from available information.

MXL is up ~30% today and ~99% over five days on 2.4x normal volume, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The overbought RSI (84.7) and unusual volume suggest a significant catalyst — possibly earnings, an M&A rumor, or options-driven momentum — but nothing in the available news confirms a specific driver.

OI dropped 15.5% on nearly 6x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The oversold RSI (25.4), bearish MACD cross, and extreme relative volume suggest a significant catalyst — possibly an earnings miss, guidance cut, or credit event — but nothing in the available news confirms it.

PLUG surged 12% on 1.83x relative volume with no identifiable news catalyst in the supplied feed. The move pushed RSI to overbought territory (70.4), suggesting the spike is likely driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation rather than a fundamental development.

POET Technologies is down nearly 18% today and almost 50% over the past five days on 4.67x normal volume, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The sustained sell-off with elevated volume could reflect post-catalyst unwinding, options expiry pressure, or institutional de-risking — none of which can be confirmed without further data.

PRCH is up 17% today with nearly 4x normal volume and no news in the supplied feed to explain it. The move is likely driven by options flow, short squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation that isn't visible in the available data.

SOFI dropped 15.5% on 3.26x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The combination of extreme volume, bearish MACD cross, and RSI approaching oversold territory suggests aggressive selling — likely driven by macro rate sensitivity, options flow, or sector rotation — but no specific catalyst is identifiable from available information.

INTC is up 12% today and 45% over the past five days on elevated but not extreme volume, with RSI deep into overbought territory at 86.6. No news in the supplied feed explains this move — the driver is likely options flow, short covering, or sector rotation not visible in this data set.

KO is up 0.66% today with a modest volume pickup (1.36x average) and a solid 5-day gain of 5.72%, but no news in the supplied feed explains either the daily move or the broader weekly run. This looks like sector rotation into defensive consumer staples or broader market flows rather than any company-specific catalyst.

NVDA fell 1.78% on slightly above-average volume with no clear news catalyst directly tied to the company. Microsoft's strong Q3 results and increased data center/AI chip investment could provide a modest tailwind for NVDA as a key supplier, but the news doesn't explain a down move of this magnitude — the dip more likely reflects normal profit-taking after a 3.4% five-day run-up.

RKLB is down ~2% today and nearly 15% over the past five days, but no news in the supplied feed explains the move. Below-average relative volume (0.58) suggests this is likely profit-taking or broader sector rotation rather than a company-specific catalyst. The bearish MACD cross confirms short-term momentum deterioration, but the cause is not identifiable from available information.

XOM is up 2.72% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The relative volume of 0.93 is below average, making a news-driven catalyst unlikely; the move is more consistent with sector rotation, crude oil price fluctuations, or broader energy market flows that aren't captured here.
